programmes.name: LEGO Spike Prime
programmes.subtitle: Robotics Foundations
programmes.description: Hands-on building and block/Python coding with the Spike Prime kit. Covers mechanical design, sensors, and basic autonomous behaviors — the ideal entry point for beginners.
programmes.outcomes: Mini robot showcase at term end

programmes.name: FLL Competition Track
programmes.subtitle: Past-Season Kit-Based
programmes.description: Real FIRST LEGO League season kits and challenge missions. Covers strategy, mission scoring, engineering notebook writing, and teamwork — the full competition experience.

programmes.name: Micro:bit Coding
programmes.subtitle: Electronics & Physical Computing
programmes.description: BBC micro:bit — block and Python coding, sensors, LEDs, radio communication, and wearable electronics projects. Physical computing made accessible and fun.

programmes.name: HTML Basics
programmes.subtitle: Intro to Web Development
programmes.description: HTML structure, basic CSS styling, and simple page-building projects. A bridge into software literacy — the internet explained from first principles. BYOD or laptop rental available.

programmes.name: AI for Kids
programmes.subtitle: Concepts, Ethics & Hands-on AI
programmes.description: Pattern recognition, training data, ethics, and kid-safe AI tools through games and projects. No heavy math — honest, grounded AI literacy for curious minds.

programmes.name: Custom Robot Build
programmes.subtitle: CAD & 3D Printing
programmes.description: CAD basics, 3D-printed custom parts, and robot assembly with original programming. For students who have outgrown standard kits and are ready to design from scratch.

subscriptions.name: Starter
subscriptions.description: Access to 1 track, online resource library. Best for beginners exploring robotics.

subscriptions.name: Explorer
subscriptions.description: Access to 2 tracks, loaner kit access, project showcase entry.

subscriptions.name: Pro / Competition
subscriptions.frequency: 3× per week · all tracks + FLL eligibility
subscriptions.description: All tracks, FLL team eligibility, monthly 3D-print credit. For competition-bound students.

faqs.question: Do I need prior experience to join?
faqs.answer: No prior experience required. Most of our tracks — LEGO Spike Prime and Micro:bit in particular — are designed specifically for complete beginners. We start from zero every term.

faqs.question: Is equipment provided or do we need to buy anything?
faqs.answer: Most hardware is provided or available as a loaner included in the course fee. HTML Basics requires a laptop — bring your own or rent one from us. The Custom Robot Build track includes a 3D-print filament credit.

faqs.question: Can my child join mid-term?
faqs.answer: Yes, for most tracks. We'll find the right entry point and settle in at the right level. Contact us and we'll advise based on the current cohort stage.

faqs.question: Do students receive certificates?
faqs.answer: Yes — every student receives a digital certificate of completion at the end of their term. FLL track students also receive their competition records and robot performance logs.
